metadata.title: Cohen–Coon PID Tuning (normalized, K=1) — First-Order-Plus-Dead-Time
notes: Classical Cohen–Coon tuning rules depend on process gain K, time constant T, and dead time L.
This file normalizes the plant gain to K=1 so it can be used with the current CLI (which accepts L and T only).
If your plant gain is not unity, scale Kp by 1/K after computing (i.e., divide the reported Kp by the actual plant gain).
Let R = L/T; many expressions below are written in L and T explicitly for clarity.

R.description: Convenience ratio R=L/T (used implicitly in formulas).

CC_step.name: Cohen–Coon step-response (normalized K=1)
CC_step.plant_model_assumption: First-order plus dead-time (FOPDT) with unit gain: e^{-L s} / (T s + 1)
